title_en: Legislature passes back-to-work legislation using the notwithstanding clause
title_fr: La législature adopte une loi de retour au travail en utilisant la clause dérogatoire
description_en: The government passes back-to-work legislation invoking section 33 of the Charter (the notwithstanding clause) to order striking teachers back to work.
inference_note: Some reporting characterized this as the first use of the notwithstanding clause to override a labour action in Canada. This 'first' framing is contested: Ontario invoked s.33 against ~55,000 education workers in 2022 (Bill 28, later repealed). Treat the s.33 back-to-work passage as fact; treat the 'first in Canada' claim as unverified/likely inaccurate.

opposition_response.name: Naheed Nenshi
opposition_response.description: Official Opposition condemned the use of the notwithstanding clause against workers as unprecedented and constitutionally irresponsible.

verification_notes: RECONCILIATION: EVT-036 is the authoritative Back to School Act record. Suppress in UI.
